item.title:
LAGARDE WARNS IRAN IMPACT WILL LAST, CLASHES WITH US
ECB President Christine Lagarde challenged US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s view that the Iran war’s economic impact will be short-lived, warning damage to energy infrastructure will have lasting effects.
The clash highlights growing US-Europe tensions as surging energy costs hit Europe harder, with inflation already jumping and recession risks rising. Meanwhile, G7 nations are preparing coordinated measures to stabilize markets.
item.description:
LAGARDE WARNS IRAN IMPACT WILL LAST, CLASHES WITH US

ECB President Christine Lagarde challenged US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s view that the Iran war’s economic impact will be short-lived, warning damage to energy infrastructure will have lasting effects.

The clash highlights growing US-Europe tensions as surging energy costs hit Europe harder, with inflation already jumping and recession risks rising. Meanwhile, G7 nations are preparing coordinated measures to stabilize markets.

item.title:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ TRAFFIC REMAINS LOW
Transit through the Strait of Hormuz is far below pre-war levels, with most vessels using a narrow northern route between Iran’s Larak and Qeshm islands.
In March, 80% of the few tankers passing through were Iranian or from friendly nations, highlighting Tehran’s growing control.
item.description:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ TRAFFIC REMAINS LOW

Transit through the Strait of Hormuz is far below pre-war levels, with most vessels using a narrow northern route between Iran’s Larak and Qeshm islands.

In March, 80% of the few tankers passing through were Iranian or from friendly nations, highlighting Tehran’s growing control.

item.description: HEGSETH: IF NO IRAN DEAL, WE'LL NEGOTIATE WITH BOMBS

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS NOT READY TO ABANDON HORMUZ BID
President Trump told CBS he isn’t ready to end U.S. efforts to force Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, despite frustration that allies haven’t contributed militarily.
He claimed Iran has been “decimated” and downplayed the threat to the strait, saying other nations should step in to secure oil themselves.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS NOT READY TO ABANDON HORMUZ BID

President Trump told CBS he isn’t ready to end U.S. efforts to force Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, despite frustration that allies haven’t contributed militarily.

He claimed Iran has been “decimated” and downplayed the threat to the strait, saying other nations should step in to secure oil themselves.

item.description: BUFFETT SAYS HE DOESN'T KNOW IF HE WOULD CUT INTEREST RATES IF HE WERE AT THE FEDERAL RESERVE

item.title:
TRUMP SLAMS FRANCE OVER AIRSPACE BLOCK
President Trump called France “VERY UNHELPFUL” after it reportedly denied US warplanes access to its airspace during the Iran war.
The dispute adds to tensions with European allies, as Spain and Italy have also restricted US military operations. France maintains it only allows US use of its bases for non-combat missions.
item.description:
TRUMP SLAMS FRANCE OVER AIRSPACE BLOCK

President Trump called France “VERY UNHELPFUL” after it reportedly denied US warplanes access to its airspace during the Iran war.

The dispute adds to tensions with European allies, as Spain and Italy have also restricted US military operations. France maintains it only allows US use of its bases for non-combat missions.

item.title:
S&P 500 MAY FALL FURTHER AS VALUATIONS COMPRESS
Wolfe Research warns stocks could drift lower as war-related risks weigh on markets, with no clear bottom yet.
They see the S&P 500’s valuation dropping to about 18x forward earnings, implying roughly 5% more downside. While near-term sentiment is weak, the long-term earnings outlook remains solid.
item.description:
S&P 500 MAY FALL FURTHER AS VALUATIONS COMPRESS

Wolfe Research warns stocks could drift lower as war-related risks weigh on markets, with no clear bottom yet.

They see the S&P 500’s valuation dropping to about 18x forward earnings, implying roughly 5% more downside. While near-term sentiment is weak, the long-term earnings outlook remains solid.

item.title: ECB'S MULLER: CAN'T RULE OUT RATE HIKE IN APRIL
item.description: ECB'S MULLER: CAN'T RULE OUT RATE HIKE IN APRIL

item.title:
OIL RISKS RISE AS HOUTHI THREATS GROW
Escalation by Yemen’s Houthi militants could push oil toward $140 a barrel, especially if the US deepens involvement in the Iran war, analysts warn.
Strikes on Red Sea shipping or Saudi energy sites would hit supply, raising inflation and slowing global growth. However, direct attacks on Saudi oil facilities are seen as unlikely unless Saudi Arabia enters the war.
item.description:
OIL RISKS RISE AS HOUTHI THREATS GROW

Escalation by Yemen’s Houthi militants could push oil toward $140 a barrel, especially if the US deepens involvement in the Iran war, analysts warn.

Strikes on Red Sea shipping or Saudi energy sites would hit supply, raising inflation and slowing global growth. However, direct attacks on Saudi oil facilities are seen as unlikely unless Saudi Arabia enters the war.

item.title: ONE OF IRAN'S QESHM ISLAND'S DESALINATION PLANTS IS OUT OF SERVICE SINCE STRIKE, SHORT-TERM REPAIRS IMPOSSIBLE - BORNA CITING HEALTH MINISTRY OFFICIAL
item.description: ONE OF IRAN'S QESHM ISLAND'S DESALINATION PLANTS IS OUT OF SERVICE SINCE STRIKE, SHORT-TERM REPAIRS IMPOSSIBLE - BORNA CITING HEALTH MINISTRY OFFICIAL

item.title:
OIL COULD HIT $200 IF HORMUZ STAYS CLOSED
Oil prices could surge to $150–$200 a barrel if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut for weeks, as the Iran war disrupts global supply.
Analysts warn ongoing losses of millions of barrels are tightening the market, and physical shortages—not political signals—will drive prices higher. Continued attacks and instability are already fueling sharp volatility.
item.description:
OIL COULD HIT $200 IF HORMUZ STAYS CLOSED

Oil prices could surge to $150–$200 a barrel if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut for weeks, as the Iran war disrupts global supply.

Analysts warn ongoing losses of millions of barrels are tightening the market, and physical shortages—not political signals—will drive prices higher. Continued attacks and instability are already fueling sharp volatility.

item.title:
TRUMP OPEN TO ENDING WAR WITHOUT REOPENING HORMUZ
President Trump is willing to end the Iran war without reopening the Strait of Hormuz, prioritizing a quick military win over restoring global shipping.
His strategy focuses on weakening Iran’s military, then pushing diplomacy or allies to handle the strait later. Critics warn this could prolong global energy disruption, drive oil prices higher, and leave trade routes vulnerable.
item.description:
TRUMP OPEN TO ENDING WAR WITHOUT REOPENING HORMUZ

President Trump is willing to end the Iran war without reopening the Strait of Hormuz, prioritizing a quick military win over restoring global shipping.

His strategy focuses on weakening Iran’s military, then pushing diplomacy or allies to handle the strait later. Critics warn this could prolong global energy disruption, drive oil prices higher, and leave trade routes vulnerable.
